analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-430

POL-430.title: Implement: Add polaris upgrade command to sync POLARIS_RULES.md
POL-430.body:
## Objective

Add a `polaris upgrade` CLI command that refreshes `POLARIS_RULES.md` in an existing managed repo from the CLI's embedded template, without requiring a full repo scan.

## Context

[POL-429](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-429/analyze-add-polaris-upgrade-command-to-sync-polaris-rulesmd-on-cli) (Analyze) identified that `POLARIS_RULES.md` is written once by `polaris adopt` and never automatically refreshed when the CLI is updated. When new skill commands are added to the routing table, managed repos miss them until someone manually re-runs adopt with `overwrite: true`. There is no upgrade path today.

The fix: a `polaris upgrade` command that (1) reads the CLI's current embedded template, (2) extracts the repo-specific `## Repository Overview` from the existing `POLARIS_RULES.md`, (3) rebuilds the file with the new template + preserved overview + version stamp, and (4) skips if the version stamp is already current.

Source analysis: `smartdocs/raw/pol-429-polaris-upgrade-command-analysis.md`

## Goal

`polaris upgrade` is fast, idempotent, safe to run in CI on every install, and requires no full repo scan. After running, `POLARIS_RULES.md` reflects the current CLI's routing table and governance rules.

## Scope

* src/cli/adopt-rules.ts
* src/cli/adopt-rules.test.ts
* src/cli/upgrade-command.ts
* src/cli/upgrade-command.test.ts
* src/cli/index.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `polaris upgrade` refreshes `POLARIS_RULES.md` from the CLI's embedded template
- [ ] Existing `## Repository Overview` content is preserved across upgrade
- [ ] A version stamp (`<!-- polaris-version: X.Y.Z -->`) is embedded in the generated file
- [ ] Command is a no-op (exits 0, prints "already up to date") when stamp matches current version
- [ ] Tests pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli/adopt-rules src/cli/upgrade-command

## Ordering

Child 1 (logic) must complete before Child 2 (CLI surface).

## Non-goals

* Do not refresh `.polaris/skills/` — skills may be customized per repo
* Do not run a full repo scan
* Do not touch agent instruction files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md) — they are already thin pointers after adoption

POL-429.title: Analyze: Add polaris upgrade command to sync POLARIS_RULES.md on CLI version update

POL-432.title: Implement: Add polaris upgrade CLI command and register in index.ts
POL-432.body:
## Objective

Wire the `polaris upgrade` CLI command using the `refreshPolarisRules` function added in [POL-431](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-431/fix-add-refreshpolarisrules-and-extractrepooverview-to-adopt-rulests).

## Context

[POL-431](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-431/fix-add-refreshpolarisrules-and-extractrepooverview-to-adopt-rulests) adds `refreshPolarisRules(repoRoot, version)` to `adopt-rules.ts`. This child creates the CLI surface: a new `upgrade-command.ts` that calls it and registers the command in `index.ts`.

The command should:

1. Resolve `repoRoot` (same pattern as other commands — default to `cwd`)
2. Call `getVersion()` from `version.ts` for the current CLI version
3. Call `refreshPolarisRules(repoRoot, version)`
4. Print `"POLARIS_RULES.md updated to version X.Y.Z"` on update, or `"Already up to date (X.Y.Z)"` on skip
5. Exit 0 in both cases; exit 1 on error

## Goal

`polaris upgrade` works end-to-end in a managed repo. Running it twice in a row is a no-op on the second call.

## Scope

* src/cli/upgrade-command.ts
* src/cli/upgrade-command.test.ts
* src/cli/index.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `polaris upgrade` runs successfully in a managed repo with an existing `POLARIS_RULES.md`
- [ ] Prints update confirmation including the version when file is refreshed
- [ ] Prints "already up to date" with version when stamp matches
- [ ] Running twice in a row: second run is a no-op
- [ ] `polaris upgrade --help` shows a useful description
- [ ] Command is registered in `index.ts` alongside `adopt` and other top-level commands
- [ ] Tests pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli/upgrade-command

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-431](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-431/fix-add-refreshpolarisrules-and-extractrepooverview-to-adopt-rulests) being Done first.

## Non-goals

* Do not implement `--force` flag (idempotent overwrite can be added later if needed).
* Do not add `--dry-run` in this pass.
* Do not refresh anything other than `POLARIS_RULES.md`.

POL-431.title: Fix: Add refreshPolarisRules and extractRepoOverview to adopt-rules.ts
POL-431.body:
## Objective

Add the core refresh logic to `adopt-rules.ts` so the upgrade command can rebuild `POLARIS_RULES.md` without a full repo scan.

## Context

`generatePolarisRules` in `adopt-rules.ts` requires a `RepoScanInventory` to build the `## Repository Overview` section. Running a full scan during upgrade is too expensive. Instead, the upgrade path should extract the existing overview from the current `POLARIS_RULES.md` and replant it into the new template.

Two additions are needed:

* `extractRepoOverview(content: string): string` — parses the `## Repository Overview` section out of an existing `POLARIS_RULES.md`. Returns a safe fallback if the section is absent or malformed.
* `refreshPolarisRules(repoRoot: string, currentVersion: string): RefreshResult` — reads the existing file, checks the embedded version stamp (`<!-- polaris-version: X.Y.Z -->`), skips if already current, otherwise extracts overview, rebuilds content via `buildContentInline`, prepends version stamp, and writes.

The version stamp format: first line of the file is `<!-- polaris-version: X.Y.Z -->`.

`buildContentInline` should remain unchanged — `refreshPolarisRules` prepends the stamp before writing.

## Goal

`refreshPolarisRules(repoRoot, version)` is exported and callable from the upgrade command. It returns `{ status: "updated" | "skipped", path: string }`.

## Scope

* src/cli/adopt-rules.ts
* src/cli/adopt-rules.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `extractRepoOverview(content)` correctly parses the `## Repository Overview` section from a valid `POLARIS_RULES.md`
- [ ] `extractRepoOverview` returns `"Repository managed by Polaris."` when the section is absent or empty
- [ ] `refreshPolarisRules` reads the existing file's version stamp and returns `{ status: "skipped" }` when it matches `currentVersion`
- [ ] `refreshPolarisRules` overwrites the file and returns `{ status: "updated" }` when the stamp is absent or differs
- [ ] The written file's first line is `<!-- polaris-version: X.Y.Z -->`
- [ ] The written file's `## Repository Overview` matches what was extracted from the original
- [ ] Unit tests cover: parse with section present, parse with section absent, skip on version match, update on version mismatch
- [ ] Tests pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli/adopt-rules

## Ordering

Must complete before POL-432 (upgrade-command.ts).

## Non-goals

* Do not modify `generatePolarisRules` or `buildContentInline`.
* Do not scan the repo filesystem.
* Do not touch agent instruction files.
